commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: The remaining action is maintainer approval of the fail-closed compatibility and execution-boundary behavior; there is no narrow automated repair to request.; Cleared: The diff tightens a Codex native execution boundary and does not add dependencies, workflow changes, secret handling, or new supply-chain execution paths. (sha=1d7aabdd995a25b5fb41d643348154853d6ae7dc)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a private Codex native-execution policy, applies it to app-server native methods, compaction, `/btw`, `/codex` controls, and bound app-server turns under effective `exec host=node`, while preserving OpenClaw dynamic `exec`/`process` and adding focused tests plus a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main has only a narrower run-attempt node-host gate, and the PR body includes AWS Crabbox before/after proof where raw Codex app-server native execution reached the wrong surface before the patch and was blocked after it.
commands.review_followup: The remaining action is maintainer approval of the fail-closed compatibility and execution-boundary behavior; there is no narrow automated repair to request.

target.title: fix(codex): honor node exec policy for native surfaces
target.body:
## Summary

- Keep Codex app-server native execution policy private to the bundled Codex plugin.
- Disable app-server-owned native execution when the effective OpenClaw exec target resolves to `host=node`, including direct app-server execution methods, native compaction, `/btw`, native `/codex` command paths, and bound app-server conversation turns.
- Preserve normal Codex harness turns by keeping OpenClaw `exec` / `process` available under `host=node`, while leaving the first-party CLI node binding path intact.

Fixes #85012

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/native-execution-policy.test.ts extensions/codex/src/app-server/request.test.ts extensions/codex/src/conversation-binding.test.ts` (`28 passed`)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-node-policy-pr-run-attempt node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts -t "node-targeted Codex app-server runs|OpenClaw exec host=node is active"` (`1 passed`, `208 skipped`)
- AWS Crabbox fix proof and regression repro: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4bba4b6a4708`, run `run_f344d939c79c`, exit `0`
- AWS Crabbox paired-node Codex harness proof: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_5ea766a7192f`, run `run_65b1ac13b54d`, exit `0`; `OPENCLAW_85012_PAIRED_NODE_CODEX_PROOF=passed`, node marker seen, gateway marker absent
- `$autoreview` on the reshaped patch: clean, no actionable findings
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Codex app-server native execution surfaces no longer bypass OpenClaw `exec host=node`; node-targeted Codex harness turns keep OpenClaw shell tools so execution routes through OpenClaw instead of Codex native app-server execution.

Real environment tested: Direct AWS Crabbox Linux environments. Policy regression proof: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_4bba4b6a4708`, run `run_f344d939c79c`. Paired-node Codex harness proof: provider `aws`, lease `cbx_5ea766a7192f`, run `run_65b1ac13b54d`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --env-from-profile /private/tmp/iao.env --allow-env OPENAI_API_KEY --script-stdin` with a script that saved the local patch, reversed it to reproduce the pre-fix behavior, reapplied it, then ran the policy proof and focused regression tests.

Evidence after fix: The regression half showed that without the patch, node-targeted runs still enabled the Codex native surface and a raw `command/exec` reached Codex native execution. After reapplying the patch, the same raw `command/exec` was blocked before app-server execution with the node-host policy message, and focused Codex policy tests passed. The paired-node proof started a real gateway plus node host, approved the node command surface, verified `system.run` was advertised, then ran a live Codex harness turn whose OpenClaw `exec` call returned the node marker and did not return the gateway marker.

Observed result after fix: The policy proof printed `OPENCLAW_85012_CODEX_NODE_POLICY_CRABBOX_PROOF=passed`; the paired-node proof printed `OPENCLAW_85012_PAIRED_NODE_CODEX_PROOF=passed`, `PROOF_NODE_MARKER_SEEN=yes`, and `PROOF_GATEWAY_MARKER_SEEN=no`; both Crabbox runs exited `0`.

What was not tested: No real paired macOS node was attached for a Linux-gateway-plus-macOS-node `Darwin` transcript. The proof verifies the broken app-server execution surface, the fixed OpenClaw block/reroute policy, and live Codex harness `exec host=node` routing through a real paired Linux node on AWS Crabbox.
target.author: joshavant

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: joshavant
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger
